name: The Horae
description: The Horae, also known as the Hours, are the Greek goddesses of the seasons and the natural portions of time. They were daughters of Zeus and Themis, representing order, justice, and law. Originally, they were personifications of the seasons, ensuring the cyclical progression of nature and the fertility of the earth. As their role expanded, they became associated with the ordering of human society, representing justice, law, and good governance. In some accounts, they are guardians of the gates of Olympus, responsible for opening and closing them. Their number varied over time, but the most common representation included three Horae: Eunomia (Order), Dike (Justice), and Eirene (Peace). These three embodied the ideals of a well-ordered society. They were often depicted as beautiful young women, adorned with flowers and fruits, symbolizing the abundance and beauty of the seasons. They were associated with dances, festivals, and the general well-being of the community, reflecting their role in maintaining harmony and prosperity. The Horae ensured the smooth transition from one season to another, preventing chaos and fostering growth. They were considered benevolent deities who brought blessings and prosperity to those who honored them. Their influence extended to the moral realm, where they upheld justice and righteousness. They were essential figures in the Greek pantheon, representing the natural and societal order that underpinned human existence.
subtitle: Goddesses of the Seasons and Order

keyMyths.title: Guardians of Olympus
keyMyths.description: The Horae serve as the gatekeepers of Olympus, opening and closing the gates for the gods. This highlights their role in maintaining order and access to the divine realm.

keyMyths.title: Daughters of Zeus and Themis
keyMyths.description: Being the offspring of Zeus and Themis, the Horae inherit their parents' qualities of power and justice. This parentage establishes their authority and importance in the pantheon.

keyMyths.title: The Seasons' Progression
keyMyths.description: The Horae ensure the smooth transition between seasons, preventing chaos and promoting fertility. This demonstrates their crucial role in the natural world and agricultural prosperity.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: Provides an account of the Horae's parentage and place in the pantheon.
sources.text: Provides an account of the Horae's parentage and place in the pantheon.

sources.author: Homeric Hymns
sources.description: Mentions the Horae's role as gatekeepers of Olympus and their association with other deities.
sources.text: Mentions the Horae's role as gatekeepers of Olympus and their association with other deities.

sources.author: Pausanias
sources.description: Describes various temples and statues dedicated to the Horae in different Greek cities.
